# Vizly — Learn Through Visuals

> Interactive visual guides and articles about system design, security, AI, and engineering concepts. Each guide features animated flows, mermaid diagrams, and step-by-step explanations.

## Learning Paths

- [Become an AI Engineer](/learning-paths/ai-engineer): A complete learning path from zero to shipping AI in production. 7 series, ~10 weeks.
- [DSA Quest](/learning-paths/dsa-quest): Learn DSA the fun way. Story-framed problems, boss fights, one pattern per dungeon. Two Pointers available now, more coming.

## Series

- [AI Fundamentals](/series/ai-fundamentals): A ground-up journey through AI, ML, deep learning, and neural networks. 6 articles.
- [Transformers & LLMs](/series/transformers-llms): From attention mechanisms to text generation — how modern language models actually work under the hood. 6 articles.
- [Post-Training & Alignment](/series/post-training-alignment): How base models become helpful assistants — supervised fine-tuning, RLHF, efficient adapters, and evaluation. 4 articles.
- [RAG, Prompting & Applications](/series/rag-prompting-applications): From crafting the perfect prompt to building retrieval-augmented systems. 4 articles.
- [Agents & Reasoning](/series/agents-reasoning): LLMs that think, plan, and act — tool-calling to multi-step reasoning agents. 4 articles.
- [Multimodal & Generative AI](/series/multimodal-generative-ai): How AI creates images, videos, and more — diffusion models to the cutting edge. 4 articles.
- [Production AI Engineering](/series/production-ai-engineering): Build, serve, evaluate, and maintain AI systems in production. 8 articles.
- [Arrays & Hashing (DSA Quest)](/series/arrays-hashing): Dungeon 1 of DSA Quest. Story-framed problems covering the foundation of every DSA journey. 7 articles.
- [Two Pointers (DSA Quest)](/series/two-pointers): Dungeon 2 of DSA Quest. Two Pointers pattern without the dry LeetCode grind. 6 articles.

## Articles

- [What is AI? From Rules to Learning](/articles/what-is-ai): AI vs ML vs DL vs GenAI, brief history, why now.
- [How Machines Learn](/articles/how-machines-learn): Supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement learning, train/test split.
- [Neural Networks from Scratch](/articles/neural-networks-from-scratch): Perceptron, layers, activation functions, forward pass.
- [Backpropagation & Training](/articles/backpropagation-and-training): Loss functions, gradient descent, learning rate, epochs.
- [CNNs & Computer Vision](/articles/cnns-and-computer-vision): Convolutions, pooling, feature maps.
- [RNNs & Sequential Data](/articles/rnns-and-sequential-data): Why sequence matters, vanishing gradients, LSTM/GRU.
- [Attention Is All You Need](/articles/attention-is-all-you-need): Self-attention, multi-head attention, why it replaced RNNs.
- [The Transformer Architecture](/articles/the-transformer-architecture): Encoder-decoder, positional encoding, layer norm, feed-forward networks.
- [Tokenization](/articles/tokenization): BPE, WordPiece, SentencePiece, vocabulary building.
- [Pre-Training LLMs](/articles/pre-training-llms): Data collection, cleaning, next-token prediction, scaling laws.
- [GPT, LLaMA & Model Families](/articles/gpt-llama-and-model-families): Decoder-only vs encoder-decoder, model zoo, parameter counts.
- [Text Generation Strategies](/articles/text-generation-strategies): Greedy, beam search, top-k, top-p, temperature.
- [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT)](/articles/supervised-fine-tuning): Instruction tuning, data formats, when to fine-tune.
- [RLHF & Reward Models](/articles/rlhf-and-reward-models): Human feedback loop, reward modeling, PPO, DPO.
- [Efficient Fine-Tuning](/articles/efficient-fine-tuning): LoRA, QLoRA, adapters, PEFT.
- [Evaluation & Benchmarks](/articles/evaluation-and-benchmarks): Perplexity, BLEU, human eval, MMLU, leaderboards.
- [Prompt Engineering](/articles/prompt-engineering): Zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought, system prompts.
- [Embeddings & Vector Search](/articles/embeddings-and-vector-search): What embeddings are, cosine similarity, vector databases, ANN indexing.
- [RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation](/articles/rag-retrieval-augmented-generation): Chunking, indexing, retrieval, generation — giving LLMs your own data.
- [Building a Chatbot](/articles/building-a-chatbot): Conversation memory, context windows, streaming, tools, guardrails.
- [AI Agents: Beyond Chat](/articles/ai-agents-beyond-chat): Agency levels, tool calling, the agent loop, chatbots vs agents.
- [Agent Patterns](/articles/agent-patterns): Chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-worker, reflection.
- [ReACT & Multi-Step Agents](/articles/react-and-multi-step-agents): Reasoning plus acting, Reflexion, tree search, multi-step planning.
- [Reasoning Models](/articles/reasoning-models): O-series, DeepSeek-R1, chain-of-thought at scale, test-time compute.
- [How Image Generation Works](/articles/how-image-generation-works): From GANs to diffusion models — how AI creates images from noise.
- [Text-to-Image (Stable Diffusion)](/articles/text-to-image-stable-diffusion): CLIP, U-Net, latent space, and sampling — turning text prompts into images.
- [Text-to-Video and Beyond](/articles/text-to-video-and-beyond): Temporal modeling, DiT architectures, and multimodal AI for video, audio, and 3D.
- [The AI Landscape: What's Next](/articles/the-ai-landscape-whats-next): Scaling laws, emergent abilities, open problems, and where AI is heading.
- [Your First AI App with HuggingFace](/articles/your-first-ai-app-with-huggingface): Loading models, running pipelines, understanding tokenizers.
- [Fine-Tuning in Practice with PyTorch](/articles/fine-tuning-in-practice-with-pytorch): The training loop, dataloaders, optimizers, and checkpoints.
- [LangChain & LlamaIndex: Build with LLMs](/articles/langchain-and-llamaindex): Chains, tools, memory, document loaders, and indexes for LLM apps.
- [Serving AI Models: From Notebook to API](/articles/serving-ai-models): FastAPI, model loading, batching, latency vs throughput.
- [Cloud AI: AWS, GCP & Azure for AI Engineers](/articles/cloud-ai-aws-gcp-azure): SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI — managed endpoints vs self-hosting.
- [MLOps: Shipping AI Like Software](/articles/mlops-shipping-ai-like-software): Experiment tracking, model registries, CI/CD for models, dataset versioning.
- [Evaluating LLMs at Scale](/articles/evaluating-llms-at-scale): LLM-as-judge, eval frameworks, red-teaming, regression testing.
- [Cost, Latency & Context Management](/articles/cost-latency-and-context-management): Token budgets, semantic caching, streaming, context window strategies.
- [Contains Duplicate — The Coat Check](/articles/contains-duplicate-coat-check): Hash set pattern, O(n²) → O(n) speedup, story-framed.
- [Valid Palindrome — The Museum Mirror](/articles/valid-palindrome-museum-mirror): Two Pointers pattern, O(1) space, early exit, story-framed.

## Guides — System Design

- [Load Balancing](/guides/load-balancing): Round-robin, least connections, IP hash, weighted strategies.
- [Caching Strategies](/guides/caching-strategies): Cache-aside, write-through, write-back, read-through.
- [CDN](/guides/cdn): Content delivery networks, edge caching, origin servers.
- [API Gateway](/guides/api-gateway): Routing, rate limiting, authentication, load balancing.
- [Rate Limiter](/guides/rate-limiter): Token bucket, sliding window, fixed window algorithms.
- [Circuit Breaker](/guides/circuit-breaker): Closed, open, half-open states for fault tolerance.
- [Service Discovery](/guides/service-discovery): Client-side vs server-side, service registries.
- [Message Queues](/guides/message-queues): Point-to-point, pub/sub, message brokers.
- [Event-Driven Architecture](/guides/event-driven-architecture): Event sourcing, CQRS, event buses.
- [Consistent Hashing](/guides/consistent-hashing): Hash rings, virtual nodes, rebalancing.
- [Proxy Servers](/guides/proxy-servers): Forward proxy, reverse proxy, load balancers.
- [CAP Theorem](/guides/cap-theorem): Consistency, availability, partition tolerance tradeoffs.
- [Microservices Architecture](/guides/microservices-architecture): Service decomposition, communication patterns.
- [Monolithic Architecture](/guides/monolithic-architecture): Single deployable unit, when monoliths make sense.
- [Serverless Architecture](/guides/serverless-architecture): FaaS, BaaS, cold starts, scaling.
- [Client-Server Architecture](/guides/client-server-architecture): Request-response model, thick vs thin clients.
- [Stateful vs Stateless](/guides/stateful-stateless-architecture): Session management, horizontal scaling implications.
- [Vertical vs Horizontal Scaling](/guides/vertical-horizontal-scaling): Scale up vs scale out strategies.

## Guides — Database

- [Database Replication](/guides/database-replication): Leader-follower, multi-leader, leaderless replication.
- [Database Sharding](/guides/database-sharding): Range, hash, directory-based sharding strategies.
- [Database Indexing](/guides/database-indexing): B-tree, hash, composite indexes, query optimization.

## Guides — Auth & Security

- [JWT](/guides/jwt): JSON Web Tokens, claims, signing, verification flow.
- [OAuth 2.0](/guides/oauth): Authorization code, implicit, client credentials flows.

## Guides — Programming

- [OOP](/guides/oop): Encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction.
- [Big O Notation](/guides/big-onotation): Time and space complexity analysis.
- [Bloom Filters](/guides/bloom-filters): Probabilistic data structures, false positives, use cases.

## About

Vizly is built by [Md Obydullah](https://github.com/mdobydullah). Source code: [github.com/mdobydullah/vizly](https://github.com/mdobydullah/vizly).
